title: Selenicereus setaceus
taxonomy: Selenicereus setaceus is classified as a species of flowering cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is a type of epiphytic cactus native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This species belongs to the subgenus Pachycereus, which includes several other epiphytic and terrestrial cacti. Selenicereus setaceus is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cactoideae, which comprises many species of cacti with cylindrical stems. At the rank of genus, Selenicereus setaceus is a member of the tribe Hylocereeae.
uses: Selenicereus setaceus, also known as the "Queen of the Night" or "Night-blooming Cereus," has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its extracts and sap have been employed to treat various conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and even certain types of cancer. Additionally, the plant is believed to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of infections and other diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and effects of Selenicereus setaceus. In some cultures, the plant's extracts are also used to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing.

intro: Selenicereus setaceus, also known as the Queen of the Night or night-blooming cereus, is a species of flowering cactus native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising remedy for treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
